q.en: At the beginning of the story, where does Jae find his missing science report?
q.zh: 在故事开头，杰是在哪里找到他丢失的科学报告的？

options.en: Leaning against a quart of milk in the refrigerator.
options.zh: 靠在冰箱里一夸脱牛奶的旁边。

options.en: On the kitchen table. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在厨房的桌子上。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Under his bed.
options.zh: 在他的床底下。

options.en: Inside his backpack.
options.zh: 在他的背包里。

explain.en: The text says, 'There, leaning against a quart of milk, was his science report.'
explain.zh: 文中说：“就在那儿，靠在一夸脱牛奶的旁边，是他的科学报告。”

q.en: Who is Ron, and what was his job?
q.zh: 罗恩是谁，他的职业是什么？

options.en: He is Jae's teacher at school.
options.zh: 他是杰的学校老师。

options.en: He is the school's umpire.
options.zh: 他是学校的裁判。

options.en: He is a famous baseball player. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他是一名著名的棒球运动员。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He is a neighbor, a good friend of Jae's dad, and a former professional umpire for thirty years.
options.zh: 他是一位邻居、杰爸爸的好朋友，也是一名干了三十年的职业裁判。

explain.en: Ron is described as 'a good friend of Dad's' who was 'a professional umpire for thirty years' before retiring.
explain.zh: 罗恩被描述为“爸爸的好朋友”，在退休前是“干了三十年的职业裁判”。

q.en: Why do you think the author named the story 'The Loser'?
q.zh: 你认为作者为什么把故事命名为《丢三落四的杰》？

options.en: Because he is a poor baseball player.
options.zh: 因为他是个很差的棒球运动员。

options.en: Because Jae keeps losing things, though each loss brings him a new friend.
options.zh: 因为杰总是丢东西，但每一次丢失都让他交到新朋友。

options.en: Because he fails a school test.
options.zh: 因为他考试不及格。

options.en: Because he loses an important baseball game. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为他输掉了一场重要的棒球比赛。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The title is ironic: Jae literally 'loses' homework, keys, a book, and a baseball, yet each loss connects him with Ron, Martina, and a puppy.
explain.zh: 标题具有反讽意味：杰确实“丢了”作业、钥匙、书和棒球，但每次丢失都让他结识了罗恩、玛蒂娜和一只小狗。

q.en: When Jae loses his dinosaur book at school, what does Martina do?
q.zh: 当杰在学校弄丢他的恐龙书时，玛蒂娜做了什么？

options.en: She helps him search his desk.
options.zh: 她帮他翻找书桌。

options.en: She tells the teacher about it. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她把这件事告诉了老师。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She lends him her own illustrated dinosaur encyclopedia.
options.zh: 她把自己那本带插图的恐龙百科借给了他。

options.en: She buys him a brand-new book.
options.zh: 她给他买了一本全新的书。

explain.en: Martina says, 'I have a really good one here... if you want to borrow mine,' and pulls out 'an illustrated dinosaur encyclopedia' — the same one Jae uses.
explain.zh: 玛蒂娜说：“我这里有一本很好的……如果你想借我的，”并拿出一本“带插图的恐龙百科”——正是杰用的那本。

q.en: How does Jae's attitude toward losing things change by the end of the story?
q.zh: 到故事结尾时，杰对丢东西的态度发生了怎样的变化？

options.en: He starts ignoring his lost things.
options.zh: 他开始无视自己丢失的东西。

options.en: He realizes losing things led to new friends, so he thinks maybe he should lose stuff more often.
options.zh: 他意识到丢东西带来了新朋友，所以想也许自己该多丢点东西。

options.en: He becomes angry and gives up.
options.zh: 他变得愤怒并放弃了。

options.en: He decides he must never lose anything again. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他决定自己绝不能再次丢东西。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: After gaining friends, Jae thinks, 'Well, maybe I should lose stuff more often,' showing a shift from frustration to appreciation.
explain.zh: 交到朋友后，杰想：“好吧，也许我该多丢点东西，”表现出从沮丧到感激的转变。

q.en: What does Jae's mother say about the stray puppy he finds?
q.zh: 杰的妈妈对他找到的流浪小狗说了什么？

options.en: 'He'll be your responsibility' — Jae must take care of him.
options.zh: “他将是你的责任”——杰必须照顾他。

options.en: Ron should keep the puppy.
options.zh: 应该由罗恩来养这只小狗。

options.en: The park owns the puppy.
options.zh: 这只小狗归公园所有。

options.en: They must return the puppy to the park. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们必须把小狗送回公园。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: His mother smiles and says, 'Yes, he does... He'll be your responsibility, though,' and Jae 'nodded excitedly.'
explain.zh: 妈妈笑着说：“是的，他确实需要……不过，他将是你的责任，”杰“兴奋地点了点头”。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing 'The Loser'?
q.zh: 作者写《丢三落四的杰》的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To warn readers about stray dogs. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 警告读者小心流浪狗。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To teach the rules of baseball.
options.zh: 教棒球的规则。

options.en: To explain how to do homework.
options.zh: 解释如何做家庭作业。

options.en: To show that losing things isn't always bad and can lead to new friendships.
options.zh: 说明丢东西并不总是坏事，还可能带来新的友谊。

explain.en: The story follows Jae turning each loss into a new friendship, ending with 'almost every time he had lost something, he had gained a friend.'
explain.zh: 故事讲述杰把每次丢失都变成了新的友谊，结尾写道“几乎每次他丢了东西，都交到了一个朋友”。

q.en: What is Jae's favorite dinosaur, and what is it like?
q.zh: 杰最喜欢的恐龙是什么，它有什么特点？

options.en: Stegosaurus — slow with plates on its back.
options.zh: 剑龙——行动缓慢，背上有板状骨。

options.en: Microraptor — tiny, but speedy.
options.zh: 小盗龙——体型很小，但速度很快。

options.en: Tyrannosaurus Rex — huge and fierce.
options.zh: 霸王龙——巨大而凶猛。

options.en: Velociraptor — a clever hunter.
options.zh: 伶盗龙——聪明的猎手。

explain.en: Jae scribbles about 'his favorite dinosaur, Microraptor,' and later tells Martina they are 'tiny, but speedy.'
explain.zh: 杰在报告中写了“他最喜欢的恐龙——小盗龙”，后来告诉玛蒂娜它们“很小，但速度很快”。

q.en: Based on the story, what kind of person is Jae?
q.zh: 根据故事，杰是个什么样的人？

options.en: Forgetful, but friendly and optimistic — he makes friends easily.
options.zh: 健忘，但友善且乐观——他很容易交到朋友。

options.en: Very shy and quiet, who avoids people.
options.zh: 非常害羞安静，回避他人。

options.en: Careless and mean to others.
options.zh: 粗心且对别人刻薄。

options.en: Perfect, neat, and never makes mistakes. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 完美、整洁，从不犯错。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Jae keeps losing items yet quickly bonds with Ron, Martina, and the puppy, showing he is forgetful but warm and hopeful.
explain.zh: 杰不断丢东西，却很快和罗恩、玛蒂娜和小狗建立了感情，说明他虽然健忘，但热情而充满希望。

q.en: The book includes a 'Social Studies' activity about losing something. Why does the author add this?
q.zh: 书中包含一个关于“丢失物品”的“社会研究”活动。作者为什么加入这个？

options.en: To summarize the plot for the reader.
options.zh: 为读者总结情节。

options.en: To teach map-reading skills.
options.zh: 教看地图的技能。

options.en: To help readers connect the story to their own experiences of losing things.
options.zh: 帮助读者把故事和自己丢失东西的经历联系起来。

options.en: To list the story's vocabulary words. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 列出故事里的词汇。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The Social Studies page asks readers to write about a time they lost something and how they reacted, linking the theme to their own lives.
explain.zh: “社会研究”页让读者写下自己曾丢失东西的一次经历及反应，把主题和他们的生活联系起来。

prompt.en: How does Jae react when he loses things? What does this say about his character? Think about a time you lost something — how did you react?
prompt.zh: 杰在丢东西时是如何反应的？这说明了他怎样的性格？想一想你自己丢东西的一次经历——你当时是怎么反应的？

en: Jae first feels frustrated ('Why can't I keep track of my stuff?') but stays calm and looks for help.
Each loss leads him to meet someone new (Ron, Martina, the puppy), showing he is friendly and open.
By the end he sees losing as a chance to gain friends, showing optimism and resilience.
zh: 杰一开始感到沮丧（“我怎么就管不住自己的东西呢？”），但保持冷静并寻求帮助。
每次丢失都让他结识新朋友，说明他友善、乐于交往。
最后他把丢失看作交朋友的机会，体现出乐观和韧性。
